ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Continued To Apr-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John O'Grady for co-administrators Joreita Yohanan and Jana Yohanan.
Ellen Leonida for beneficiary John D. Yohanan.
Avid Alavi for proposed successor administrator David Levin (via Zoom).
Seth Skootsky for Jana Yohanan in her individual capacity.
Jana Yohanan, co-administrator (via Zoom).
John Yohanan, beneficiary (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Status on Close of Estate; 2) Attorney John O'Grady's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Co-Administrators; and 3) Petition for Suspension and Removal.

Ms. Leonida reported recent discussions regarding a proposed resolution of the Petition for Suspension and Removal, including a proposed allocation of attorney's fees and the potential appointment of a successor administrator. Ms. Leonida noted that no proposed order had yet been submitted.

Mr. O'Grady confirmed the substance of the proposal described by Ms. Leonida and advised that he had filed a petition for attorney's fees, which was set for hearing on April 20, 2026.

Mr. Skootsky stated that he had recently become involved in the matter and clarified that he represented Jana Yohanan solely in her individual capacity. Mr. Skootsky stated that he was not aware that Ms. Yohanan had agreed to the proposed resolution and addressed issues related to the status of estate income tax returns and preparation of an accounting.

Ms. Alavi advised the Court that David Levin was willing to serve as successor administrator and addressed procedural issues related to appointment.

The Court heard further argument regarding the status of estate administration, the proposed removal of the co-administrators, and accounting issues.

The Court continued the status on close of estate and the motion of attorney John O'Grady to be relieved as counsel to April 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to coincide with the hearing on Mr. O'Grady's fee petition. The Court took the Petition for Suspension and Removal under submission with intent to grant, subject to receipt of a proposed order.

The Court directed that the proposed order include a requirement that an accounting be prepared covering the period from the decedent's date of death through the date of removal, to be filed within sixty (60) days of removal. Issues concerning administrator compensation were deferred to final distribution or further proceedings.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Nov-24-2025 Continued To Dec-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John O'Grady for co-administrators Joreita Yohanan and Jana Yohanan (also present).
Ellen Leonida for beneficiary John D. Yohanan, who was also present.
Avid Alavi for proposed successor administrator David Levin, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Status on Close of Estate; 2) Petition by John O'Grady to be Relieved as Counsel; and 3) Peittion for Suspension and Removal of Co-Administrators and Appointment of a Successor Administrator.

Discussion was held regarding statutory fee allocation. Ms. Alavi stated that the estimated statutory fee is approximately $163,000, and that Mr. O'Grady has already exceeded that amount in ordinary services, with additional extraordinary fee claims anticipated. She explained that Mr. O'Grady's proposed allocation, based on hours already expended, would leave a successor attorney uncompensated for ordinary work. Mr. O'Grady stated that statutory fees carry risk, that he has performed significant work over many years, and that the fee base may increase with receipts or sale of assets.

Concerns were raised by Ms. Leonida about lack of financial information, tax filings, and delays by the co-administrators. She asserted that without access to information and a workable compensation structure, no successor attorney can complete administration.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 8, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow counsel time to meet and confer regarding fee structure and administration transition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

The matter came on for hearing on status regarding closing of the estate and on counsel John O'Grady's motion to be relieved as counsel.

Ms. Leonida reported that, despite the Court's prior instructions setting an August 27, 2025 deadline for an informal accounting, no accounting was provided. She stated that although Mr. O'Grady had indicated a bookkeeper would be hired and information produced, that had not occurred. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Civil
Off Calendar Apr-29-2024 Continued To Sep-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady appeared by zoom for and with Jana Yohanan by zoom and for Jovita Yohanan (personal representatives).

Mrs. Yohanan stated that she tried to get another attorney, but there seems to be an issue with the statutory fees and that so far two attorneys have turned it down. Mrs. Yohanan also stated that Mr. O'Grady proposed his attorney's fees, that she made a business judgment based on information that she got, that they planned to close out the estate back in 2021, but she wanted Mr. O'Grady to reduce his fees and it has gone downhill ever since. The court explained the sharing of the statutory fees between attorneys and noted that there isn't much left to carve out. Mr. O'Grady stated that he needs the court's help to close out this estate. The court stated that disputes can be presented to the court, and it can decide it. Mr. O'Grady stated that it's more than just the fees, that he needs things and that it's not happening. The court informed the parties that a note to itself raised the issue of suspending the administrators and suggested that the parties take two months to see if they can work on things. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel, and status on close of estate are continued to September 16, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Civil
Off Calendar Apr-08-2024 Continued To Apr-29-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady appeared by zoom for Jana Yohanan (sister of the decedent) and Joreita Yohanan (mother of the decedent).

The court stated that this case has been pending since 2016, that it is a medium to a large estate, and that it is a bad sign when an attorney wants out of a case and a worse sign when a party won't sign a substitution of attorney. Mr. O'Grady stated that he wants to withdraw because he is not able to make any progress, that he knows his clients are interviewing other counsel, that this would be a third attorney and expressed concern about other counsel not wanting to take this case due to the split of the statutory fees. Mr. O'Grady also stated Jana is the one who is handling everything, that Joreita is in her 80's and asked the court not to order her to appear. An Order to Appear will be issued to the co-administrator Jana Yohana to appear on April 29, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to address the court's concerns. Joreita Yohanan is welcome to attend the hearing, but is not required to do so. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel, and status on close of estate are continued to April 29, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Nguyen. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady appeared by zoom for Joreita Yohanan and Jana Yohanan.

The Court questions why there is no petition for final distribution. Mr. O'Grady stated that they are in the midst of an estate tax audit with the IRS, that they have it narrowed down to one issue, that they are in agreement with the IRS to resolve this in the next two weeks and that they need time to allocate the estate taxes amongst the beneficiaries so that they can petition for final distribution. Mr. O'Grady also stated that they need to amend the tax return for the sale of the mobile home, that the assets are mostly real estate and there is not a lot of cash, and that the taxes were filed late. The Court stated that there is nothing in writing indicating what Mr. O'Grady has been doing to administer the estate, and informed Mr. O'Grady that he needs to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration at least one week prior to the next hearing. Status on close of estate is continued to February 14, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-13-2019 Continued To Apr-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady for Joreita Yohanan and Jana Yohanan.
Todd Christensen (Bond Agent) also present.

The Court stated that once John became an heir, he needed to waive bond. Mr. O'Grady stated that it never occurred to him that the disclaimer would cause an issue of bond, that he needs to get a waiver of bond, but needs to get the accounting done first, and that they are not there yet. Mr. O'Grady also stated that there were years of unfiled taxes for the business, that the Beneficiary is the Decedent's Brother, that the Administrators are the Decedent's Sister and Mother, that the Brother is non-cooperative, that he has signed a waiver of accountings and a waiver of bond, but he does not have the originals. The Court informed Counsel that he either needs to get the waiver or post bond. Mr. O'Grady stated that the bonding company a bond until an accounting is done. Mr. Christensen explained that if they do not issue a bond at the beginning, they will be bonding liability. The Court expressed concern stating that this needs to get resolved. The Third Report on Status of Administration is continued to April 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a final continuance. The Court informed Counsel that by that time either a waiver of bond needs to be filed, or bond is to be posted, or the Court will consider suspension.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
